algae-2006-21-4-433.json.true: The addition of P+EDTA, NO3, and Mn to the Carex culture medium increased Chl-a concentrations to 3.424 µg/L, 3.404 µg/L, and 3.630 µg/L respectively, resulting in more than a two-fold increase in growth rates compared to the untreated culture medium (1.498 µg/L).

The morphological alterations caused by heavy metal exposure in marine diatoms were investigated by Brown et al. (2002).

algae-2007-22-2-117.json.true: The zonation of the intertidal zone includes species such as Porphyra spp., Gloiopeltis furcata, Caulacanthus okamurae, Gelidium divaricatum, Neorhodomela aculeata, Enteromorpha compressa, and Ulva pertusa.

The average biomass of macroalgae in the intertidal zone was measured at 129.4 g∙dry wt/m2.
The crustacean red algal population in spring dominated the subtidal zonation, followed by Zostera marina and Laminaria japonica.

algae-2007-22-2-117.json.true: The algal zonation of the intertidal zone was determined by Porphyra spp., Gloiopeltis furcata, Caulacanthus okamurae, Gelidium divaricatum - Neorhodomela aculeata, Enteromorpha compressa - Ulva pertusa, Dumontia simplex, Laminaria japonica from upper to lower zone.

The intertidal zone showed 26 species of algae in winter, 34 in spring, 18 in summer and 18 in autumn, with Laminaria japonica, Neorhodomela aculeata and Ulva pertusa as the dominant species.

The study found 34 species in winter, 26 in spring, 18 in summer and 18 in autumn, with Porphyra spp., Gloiopeltis furcata, Caulacanthus okamurae, Gelidium divaricatum - Neorhodomela aculeata, Enteromorpha compressa - Ulva pertusa, Dumontia simplex, Laminaria japonica as the dominant species.

algae-2007-22-4-305.json.true: The flora of marine benthic algal species investigated in the uninhabited islands of Dochodo, southwestern coast of Korea, could be classified into six functional groups: coarsely branched form (39.6%), filamentous form (25.8%), sheet form (14.1%), thick leathery form (9.9%), jointed calcarious form (5.6%) and crustose form algae (4.9%).
false: The flora investigated could be classified into five functional groups such as coarsely branched form (39.6%), filamentous form (25.8%), sheet form (14.1%), thick leathery form (9.9%) and crustose form algae (4.9%).

The flora of marine benthic algal species investigated in the uninhabited islands of Dochodo, northeastern coast of Japan, could be classified into six functional groups: coarsely branched form (39.6%), filamentous form (25.8%), sheet form (14.1%), thick leathery form (9.9%), jointed calcarious form (5.6%) and crustose form algae (4.9%).

The flora of marine benthic algal species investigated in the uninhabited islands of Dochodo, southwestern coast of Korea, could be classified into six functional groups: coarsely branched form (38.6%), filamentous form (24.8%), sheet form (15.1%), thick leathery form (10.9%), jointed calcarious form (6.6%) and crustose form algae (5.9%).

algae-2009-24-2-057.json.true: The genus is characterized by having short cylindrical cells, two stellate chloroplasts per cell, a prominent central pyrenoid in each chloroplast, nucleus between chloroplasts, non-flagellated amoeboid gametes, sexual reproduction by ladder-like or lateral conjugation, and zygospore formation either in a conjugation tube or within one gametangium (Transeau 1934, 1951).
false: Approximately two hundred species have been described worldwide (Kützing 1843; Yamagishi 1965; Kadlubowska 1984; Johnson 2002; Novis 2004).

The genus is distinguished by long, spiral cells, one stellate chloroplast per cell, a small pyrenoid in each chloroplast, nucleus outside the chloroplasts, flagellated motile gametes, and asexual reproduction via binary fission (Transeau 1951).
Around one hundred twenty species have been identified globally (Bold and Wynne 1985; Yamagishi 1965; Kadlubowska 1984; Johnson 2002).

algae-2009-24-2-057.json.true: The paper mentions the description of vegetative cell width, spore shape and size, and ornamented median spore wall by Cleve (1868) and the observation of both zygospores and aplanospores by Transeau (1915, 1951).

algae-2014-29-4-333.json.true: The compound 5β-hydroxypalisadin B demonstrated a maximum of 90% inhibition on LPS-induced NO production at 50 µM and an IC50 value of 17.56 µM.

algae-2022-37-9-2.json.true: Coolia species do not show a preference for any specific macrophyte substrate in the Gulf of Mexico and the Mexican Caribbean.
